context: Tests whether the agent correctly upgrades a Shopify app from development-only defaults to production-ready configuration, including persistent session storage, pinned API versioning, and proper App Bridge initialization in the root layout.

checklist.name: Prisma session storage import
checklist.description: app/shopify.server.ts imports PrismaSessionStorage from @shopify/shopify-app-session-storage-prisma (not the SQLite variant)

checklist.name: PrismaClient instantiation
checklist.description: app/shopify.server.ts creates a PrismaClient instance and passes it to PrismaSessionStorage

checklist.name: SQLite removed
checklist.description: app/shopify.server.ts does NOT import or use SQLiteSessionStorage

checklist.name: Pinned API version
checklist.description: app/shopify.server.ts uses a specific version string (e.g. "2025-01" or similar date-based version) instead of LATEST_API_VERSION for the apiVersion field

checklist.name: LATEST_API_VERSION not in production config
checklist.description: app/shopify.server.ts does NOT pass LATEST_API_VERSION as the apiVersion value

checklist.name: AppProvider in root layout
checklist.description: app/root.tsx imports and renders AppProvider (from @shopify/shopify-app-remix/react or equivalent Shopify provider)

checklist.name: Root layout wraps Outlet
checklist.description: app/root.tsx wraps <Outlet /> inside the AppProvider component

checklist.name: Deployment notes: session storage reason
checklist.description: DEPLOYMENT_NOTES.md explains why SQLite is unsuitable for multi-instance deployments (e.g., sessions not shared across instances, file-based storage)

checklist.name: Deployment notes: API version reason
checklist.description: DEPLOYMENT_NOTES.md explains why LATEST_API_VERSION is not safe for production (e.g., may introduce breaking changes without notice)

checklist.name: Deployment notes: AppProvider reason
checklist.description: DEPLOYMENT_NOTES.md mentions that AppProvider is required for App Bridge context (e.g., useAppBridge returning null without it)
